title: Store L3 embeddings on write so the hot tier can be searched semantically
current_state: memory_write computes+stores an embedding ONLY for L2 (compute_embedding_for_write -> metadata['embedding'], tools_memory.py ~1036-1046). L3 rows (l3_cache SQLite table) carry no embedding, so L3 — the tier injected into the prompt every turn — can only be matched by BM25 lexical search (_hybrid_search_l3). Paraphrased queries ('my model organism') never hit a lexically-different stored fact ('zebrafish') at the hot tier.
target_state: L3 writes persist an embedding alongside the row: a new nullable column l3_cache.embedding (TEXT, JSON-encoded vector), created by an idempotent migration in _init_sqlite. On L3 write, store json.dumps(embedding) when an embedding provider is available; leave NULL otherwise. Fully best-effort — zero behavior change when embeddings are unavailable (offline/no key).
migration_plan: 1. In _init_sqlite, add an idempotent 'ALTER TABLE l3_cache ADD COLUMN embedding TEXT' guarded by a PRAGMA table_info check (match the existing invalid_at/pinned migration pattern).

2. In the L3 write path, compute_embedding_for_write(content) and, when non-None, store json.dumps(vector) into the embedding column. Respect the existing deferral note at ~line 911 ('embedding pass deferred to avoid blocking writes') — do NOT add a blocking network call to the hot write path; reuse whatever async/deferred hook L2 already uses, or write NULL now and fill via the backfill in step 3.
3. Add a small backfill helper backfill_l3_embeddings(limit) that embeds rows where embedding IS NULL (idempotent, capped, best-effort) for existing caches.
4. Unit test: with the embedding engine mocked to return a fixed vector, write an L3 fact via memory_write(tier='l3') and assert the l3_cache row's embedding column is a non-null JSON vector.
5. python scripts/verify.py must PASS (including the L3 roundtrip contract test).
risk_notes: Write latency is the trap: embedding is a network call and L3 writes are on the interactive path. Do NOT block the turn on it. Preferred: store NULL at write time and let backfill/the L2 async path fill it, OR only embed when a provider is configured AND the deferral hook exists. The L3 roundtrip contract test (test_memory_roundtrip_contract.py) must stay green — the new column is additive/nullable, so it should. Keep the diff to: one migration + one store line + one backfill helper + one test.
